🌊 Safe Harbor Behavioral Health
Credentialing Guide — Tulsa, Oklahoma
No Child Is Broken 💙
🚨
URGENT — Do This Today: Apollo's malpractice insurance EXPIRED February 28, 2026. You CANNOT submit any insurance applications until this is renewed. Call your liability carrier right now and request a new Certificate of Insurance.
🗺️

Your Step-by-Step Credentialing Guide

Each step tells you exactly what to do AND warns you about the most common mistakes people make — so you don't lose weeks of time finding out the hard way. One step at a time. You've got this, Adam! 💪

🎯 Your Progress
0 of 9 steps done
🪤
This guide protects you from 15 known traps Real mistakes that real clinics made — that delayed their first payment by months. Each step shows you what to watch out for.
🔵 Phase 1 — Foundation (Start Here!)
📋
Gather Clinician Information
Collect info for Lashauna & Apollo before touching any website
👆 Start Here
💡 Think of this like packing a suitcase before a trip. Get everything ready BEFORE you open any website. If you stop mid-form to hunt for a license number, the page can time out and you'll have to start all over.

📝 Collect These for EACH Clinician

— Lashauna —
  • Full legal name — exactly as it appears on her state license
  • Date of birth
  • Social Security Number
  • Home mailing address
  • Personal email address (NOT the Safe Harbor email)
  • License type (LPC, LCSW, LMFT, etc.) and license number
  • License expiration date
  • Malpractice carrier name, policy number, expiration date
  • Full work history — every job for the last 10 years with exact month and year start/end dates
— Apollo —
  • Full legal name — exactly as it appears on his state license
  • Date of birth
  • Social Security Number
  • Home mailing address
  • Personal email address (NOT the Safe Harbor email)
  • License type and license number
  • License expiration date
  • 🚨 NEW malpractice certificate (must be renewed first!)
  • Full work history — every job for the last 10 years with exact month and year start/end dates
🪤 Trap #1 — The Work History Gap Trap
⚠️CAQH requires a complete work history with ZERO unexplained gaps going back 10 years. Any gap longer than 30 days must have a written explanation. "Between jobs June–August 2022" is fine — leaving it blank is NOT. Unexplained gaps can add 6–12 weeks to your approval.
📅CAQH needs month AND year for every job start and end date. "Spring 2021" is not acceptable. Ask your clinicians for exact dates now — don't guess.
💡If a clinician was in school, on maternity leave, caring for a family member, or between jobs — that's fine! Just write it down with the dates.
🪪
Get NPI Numbers for Both Clinicians
Free government website — 20 min per person
To Do
🪪 What is an NPI? It's like a Social Security Number for a healthcare provider. Every insurance company in America requires one before they'll pay a claim. It's free and takes about 20 minutes.

🖥️ Website

🌐 Open NPPES Website

👣 Do This For Each Clinician

  • Click "Create Account" — use the clinician's PERSONAL email, not Safe Harbor's
  • Verify the email confirmation they receive
  • Click "Apply for NPI" → choose INDIVIDUAL (NOT Organization)
  • Type their legal name EXACTLY as it appears on their state license
  • Enter Safe Harbor's address as the practice location
  • Enter the correct taxonomy code (ask Adam if unsure)
  • Submit and WRITE DOWN the NPI number you receive
  • Save the confirmation email — do not delete it
🪤 Traps #2 & #3 — Name Mismatch + Wrong NPI Type
🔤Trap #2 — Name Mismatch: The name you enter in NPPES must be identical to the name on the state license. If the license says "Jennifer Marie Smith" but you type "Jennifer Smith" — that mismatch will cause rejections at EVERY payer down the line. Even one extra middle name causes problems.
☑️Trap #3 — Wrong NPI Type: Always click INDIVIDUAL (Type 1). If you accidentally choose Organization (Type 2), you've created the wrong kind of NPI and it takes weeks to fix. Safe Harbor's organization NPI is already done — these are for the individual people.
🔢Trap #4 — Wrong Taxonomy Code: The taxonomy code tells insurance what KIND of provider this person is. A wrong code means they might pay the wrong rate — or reject claims entirely. Confirm the exact code before submitting.
✅ You'll get the NPI number instantly after submitting. Save it somewhere safe — you'll need it for every single payer application.
📁
Build CAQH ProView Profiles
The master credentialing file — the most important step
To Do
📁 What is CAQH? Think of it as a master file about each clinician. Almost every insurance company pulls from here instead of asking for the same papers 10 times. You fill it out ONCE and share it with everyone. But it must be 100% complete — even 99% means nothing.

🖥️ Website & Phone

🌐 Open CAQH ProView 📞 CAQH Help: 888-599-1771

📄 Documents to Upload

📄 Lashauna's state license ✅ Current
📄 Apollo's state license ✅ Current
🛡️ Lashauna's malpractice certificate ✅ Good til Sept 2026
🛡️ Apollo's malpractice certificate 🚨 RENEW FIRST
📋 CV / Resume for each clinician ⚠️ Confirm current
📝 W-9 for each clinician ✅ Have it

✅ After Filling Out CAQH

  • All sections are 100% complete — no blank fields anywhere
  • Clicked the ATTEST button at the end
  • Authorized EVERY payer you want to work with to access the profile
  • Set a phone reminder to re-attest every 90 days
🪤 Traps #5, #6 & #7 — The CAQH Killers
💯Trap #5 — Not 100% Complete: Research shows that 60–70% of credentialing delays come from incomplete CAQH profiles. A profile that's 95% done is the same as 0% done — payers will NOT review it until every single field is filled. Never leave a field blank. If it doesn't apply, type "N/A".
📅Trap #6 — Forgetting to Attest: CAQH requires you to click "Attest" every 120 days to confirm the info is still accurate. If you miss it, your profile goes inactive. Some insurers treat an inactive profile as a withdrawn application — meaning you start over from scratch. Set a recurring phone alarm right now.
🔓Trap #7 — Not Authorizing Payers: Filling out CAQH is not enough. You must also go into the CAQH portal and explicitly give EACH payer permission to view the profile. If you don't do this, payers literally cannot see it — and they won't tell you that's why they're stuck.
💰Trap #8 — Wrong Malpractice Numbers: In CAQH, there's a field for malpractice coverage amounts. One box says "Per Occurrence" and one says "Aggregate." A common mistake is entering the wrong number of zeros — like $100,000 instead of $1,000,000. That one missing zero causes automatic rejections at most payers.
📤Trap #9 — Uploading Expired Documents: If a document will expire during the credentialing process (which takes 3–6 months), upload the renewed version now. A payer reviewing your file in 4 months will see an expired document and deny you. Check every expiration date before uploading.
⏰ Set these phone reminders right now:
• Every 90 days — "Re-attest CAQH for Lashauna"
• Every 90 days — "Re-attest CAQH for Apollo"
• 60 days before each license expiration
• 60 days before each malpractice policy expiration
🟢 Phase 2 — Oklahoma Medicaid (SoonerSelect)
🏥
OHCA SoonerCare Group Enrollment
Oklahoma base Medicaid — 30 to 90 days to process
To Do
🏥 Safe Harbor is already approved as an organization. Now you need to enroll both clinicians as individual rendering providers under the group. This is different from the organization enrollment — it's a separate step.
🌐 OHCA Portal 📞 OHCA: 405-522-7171

📞 Call Script — What To Say

"Hi, my name is Adam James. I'm the owner of Safe Harbor Behavioral Health in Tulsa, Oklahoma. Our group NPI is 9876543210 and our Tax ID is [YOUR EIN]. We're already approved for Oklahoma Medicaid as an organization. I need to enroll two individual clinicians as rendering providers under our group. Can you walk me through that process?"
🪤 Trap #10 — Enrolling as Individual Instead of Under the Group
🏢When you enroll clinicians with OHCA, you must enroll them under Safe Harbor's group NPI — not as standalone individuals. If they enroll independently, claims will be rejected because the billing NPI won't match the group. Always tell OHCA you are adding rendering providers to an existing group.
📋Always ask for a confirmation number at the end of every call. Write it down immediately. OHCA will not track your call — only you can prove it happened.
💊
SoonerSelect — 3 Separate Insurance Plans
Humana + Aetna Better Health + Oklahoma Complete Health
To Do
⚠️ This surprises most new clinics: Oklahoma Medicaid (SoonerCare/SoonerSelect) runs through 3 separate private insurance companies called MCOs. Even though you're approved for base Medicaid, you must apply to all 3 plans individually. Missing even one means you can't be paid by patients in that plan.

Plan 1 — Humana Healthy Horizons

🌐 Humana Portal 📞 855-223-9868
  • Call and ask for the provider contracting department
  • Request a group contract application for behavioral health
  • Submit and save confirmation number

Plan 2 — Aetna Better Health of Oklahoma

🌐 Aetna Better Health 📞 844-365-4385
  • Submit application through Availity portal
  • Save confirmation number

Plan 3 — Oklahoma Complete Health

🌐 OK Complete Health 📞 833-752-1664
  • Submit through their provider portal
  • Save confirmation number
🪤 Trap #11 — Assuming OHCA Approval Covers the MCOs
❌This is the #1 Oklahoma-specific mistake. Providers think being approved for base SoonerCare (OHCA) means they're automatically in all 3 MCO plans. They are completely separate. You must apply to each MCO individually or you will get paid nothing when a patient's card says "Humana" or "Oklahoma Complete Health."
📞After submitting to each MCO, follow up every 7 days by phone. Payers do not call you when something is wrong — they just let your application sit. If you don't follow up, nothing moves.
🟣 Phase 3 — Commercial Insurance (Start These ASAP!)
💙
BCBS Oklahoma
Biggest commercial payer in Oklahoma — 90 to 120 days
To Do
💙 BCBS Oklahoma is the most important commercial payer in the state. It takes 90–120 days to process, so start this one as soon as CAQH is complete — even while other things are still in progress.
🌐 BCBS Oklahoma Portal 📞 800-942-5837
  • Log into CAQH first — authorize BCBS Oklahoma to access the profile
  • Submit group application via BCBSOK portal
  • Write down the confirmation number
  • Set a follow-up reminder for exactly 7 days from submission
🪤 Traps #12 & #13 — Not Following Up + Not Authorizing CAQH
📵Trap #12 — Payers Won't Call You: This is the most important thing to know about commercial payers. When something is wrong with your application — a missing document, a question, a discrepancy — payers do NOT call you. They just put your file on hold and wait. If you don't follow up by phone every 7–14 days, your file could sit for months doing nothing.
🔓Trap #13 — CAQH Not Authorized for BCBS: Before submitting to BCBS, you must go into CAQH and specifically click to authorize BCBS Oklahoma access. If you forget this step, BCBS will tell you the application is "pending CAQH verification" forever — because they literally can't see the file.
🟠
Aetna Commercial
Submit through Availity — 90 to 120 days
To Do
🌐 Availity Portal 📞 800-624-0756
  • Log into Availity (availity.com)
  • Find Aetna Commercial credentialing application
  • Submit and write down confirmation number
  • Set 7-day follow-up reminder
🪤 Trap #14 — Confusing Aetna Better Health with Aetna Commercial
⚠️Aetna has TWO separate networks in Oklahoma — Aetna Better Health (Medicaid/SoonerSelect) and Aetna Commercial (private insurance). They are completely different applications, different portals, and different approval processes. You must apply to both separately.
🟡
UnitedHealthcare & Cigna
Start these after BCBS & Aetna are submitted
To Do

UnitedHealthcare / Optum

🌐 UHC Portal 📞 800-711-4555
  • Submit group application and write down confirmation number
  • Set 7-day follow-up reminder

Cigna / Evernorth

🌐 Cigna Portal 📞 800-882-4462
  • Submit group application and write down confirmation number
  • Set 7-day follow-up reminder
🪤 Trap #15 — Starting Too Late
📆Commercial payers take 90–120 days to process. That means every week you wait is a week you push back your first payment. The biggest mistake new clinics make is waiting until everything else is "perfect" before submitting commercial applications. Submit as soon as CAQH is complete — don't wait for Medicaid approvals first.
💚 Phase 4 — Get Paid!
💰
Set Up Electronic Payments (EFT/ERA)
So insurance money goes straight to Safe Harbor's bank account
To Do
💰 This is the finish line! After each payer approves you, set up EFT (Electronic Funds Transfer) so payments go directly to Safe Harbor's bank account instead of paper checks. Do this separately for each payer after they approve you.

📄 What You'll Need

🏦 Voided check OR bank letter from Safe Harbor's business account
📝 Safe Harbor W-9 (group)
🆔 Group NPI: 9876543210
Most payers use Availity for EFT setup. Need help? Call Availity: 800-282-4548
  • BCBS Oklahoma EFT enrolled
  • Aetna Commercial EFT enrolled
  • Humana EFT enrolled
  • UnitedHealthcare EFT enrolled
  • Cigna EFT enrolled
  • Oklahoma Complete Health EFT enrolled
  • TherapyNotes configured with all payer IDs
🪤 Bonus Trap — Forgetting EFT After Approval
💸Many clinics get approved by payers and then forget to set up EFT. Claims go through fine — but payments go to the wrong place or get held up as paper checks. Set up EFT within 48 hours of each approval letter arriving. Don't let money sit unclaimed.
🎉

YOU DID IT, ADAM!

Safe Harbor is fully credentialed.
You navigated every trap.
Children in Tulsa are about to get the help they deserve. 💙

Mission: No Child Is Broken. ✅
